L1.area: Data marshalling between the scripting engine and the host language runtime

L2.description: A heap overflow vulnerability exists where a buffer is allocated based on an initial collection size, but iteration over the collection uses a dynamically re-evaluated length that may have grown since allocation, leading to out-of-bounds writes and potential heap corruption.

L3.area: Array value conversion routines in the native C++ extension for the embedded JavaScript engine
L3.description: A heap overflow vulnerability exists where a buffer for array elements is allocated based on the initial array length at the time of allocation. However, the loop that iterates over the array during value conversion re-queries the live array length on each iteration, which may have grown due to side effects triggered during element access. This causes writes beyond the allocated buffer, leading to out-of-bounds memory corruption.

ground_truth.reason: The array conversion routine allocates a fixed-size buffer based on the initial array length but iterates using the live array length, which can grow during conversion, causing heap overflow via out-of-bounds writes.
